summary: Scorching Ramble On Rose, tight Jack Straw, soaring Attics→Not Fade Away
blurb: The first set erupts with high‑energy rock highlighted by a scorching Ramble On Rose and a razor‑tight Jack Straw. The second half builds through a seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue into an epic Attics Of My Life → Not Fade Away climax, closing with a lively U.S. Blues encore.
review: On September 10, 1993 at the Richfield Coliseum, the Dead delivered one of their most electrifying late‑career shows. Jerry was “going off” all night – his lead guitar on Ramble On Rose blazed like a lightning bolt and his vocal delivery was front‑row fire. Phil laid down a monster bass line that drove the band forward, while Vince’s keyboards added just enough texture without ever crowding the mix. Bill and Mickey kept a rock‑solid groove, lifting the first set especially during Jack Straw and the early Terrapin‑style jams. The second set shone with the classic China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition, then surged through an inspired Attics Of My Life into a crowd‑chanting Not Fade Away that felt like a communal climax. Even the encore’s U.S. Blues landed with the right mix of humor and power, making this a show Deadheads will want to replay again and again.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings (two positive, one mixed) consistently praise the band’s high‑energy performance, standout songs and tight playing, with no recurring negative comments. The consensus of strong positive language across sources warrants a solid +1.5 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crystal‑clear mix, minimal hiss, and full band balance; showcases the show’s high energy and Jerry’s scorching guitar tone.

key_highlights: Scorching Ramble On Rose that puts Jerry’s guitar on fire
Tight Jack Straw opening that sets a high‑octane tone
Seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue
Epic Attics Of My Life → Not Fade Away climax with crowd chanting

band_performance.Jerry: Blazing lead guitar throughout, especially on Ramble On Rose and the Not Fade Away climax; vocal delivery charged with energy.
band_performance.Phil: Monster bass work that was tight, driving and propelled the band forward all night.
band_performance.Vince: Solid keyboard textures and tasteful solos that added depth without overpowering the mix.
band_performance.Mickey: Steady groove alongside Bill, keeping the rhythmic foundation rock‑solid.
band_performance.Billy: Tight drumming that lifted the first set, particularly on Jack Straw and early jams.
